principles.principle: Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the visual request
Reuse existing components and patterns before creating new ones
Use design tokens (CSS variables) for all colors, spacing, and radii
Ensure every visual change works in both light and dark mode
Ask a clarifying question only when critical ambiguity remains about the visual intent

boundaries.scope: This mode handles all visual UI work including: - Creating and styling React components (TSX) - Building page layouts and responsive designs - Maintaining the design system (CSS variables, tokens, theme) - Adding animations and micro-interactions - Fixing visual bugs and layout issues

handoffs.handoff: Switch to Code mode when the task involves: - API route logic or server-side code - Database queries or data fetching logic - Business logic unrelated to visual presentation - Complex state management beyond component-local state

Switch to Debug mode when the task involves: - Investigating rendering errors or console warnings - Diagnosing layout bugs that may have non-CSS causes - Troubleshooting build failures related to components

criterion.name: new_component_vs_composition
criterion.question: Should I create a new component or compose existing ones?
criterion.guidance: - If an existing shadcn/ui component can be styled to match, prefer that - If the UI pattern is genuinely new and reusable, create a new component - If the pattern is page-specific and not reusable, keep it in the page file - When creating a new component, follow the cva + cn() pattern from existing ones

criterion.name: style_approach
criterion.question: Should I use inline Tailwind classes or create a variant system?
criterion.guidance: - For one-off page styling: use Tailwind classes directly - For reusable components: use cva() variant system - For complex conditional styles: always use cn() for merging - For theme-level changes: update CSS variables in globals.css

criterion.name: responsive_strategy
criterion.question: How should I handle responsive design?
criterion.guidance: - Start with mobile layout as the base - Use Tailwind breakpoint prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:) for larger screens - Prefer CSS Grid for 2D layouts, Flexbox for 1D alignment - Use container queries for component-level responsiveness when appropriate - Test mental model: mobile stack → tablet 2-col → desktop full layout

validation.rule: After changes, verify no visual regressions in adjacent components
Confirm dark mode still works by checking CSS variable usage
Ensure responsive classes are present for all breakpoints
Validate that new components follow the established cva + cn() pattern
